title: pseudoeurystyles gardneri
intro: Pseudoeurystyles gardneri, also known as the 'Sarangalia', is a plant species native to tropical regions, particularly in the Himalayas and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with its roots, leaves, and stems being used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting overall well-being. In traditional herbal preparations, Pseudoeurystyles gardneri is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or ointments, and is also used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat a range of health ailments.
